segments.speaker: Unknown
segments.raw_text:
- Strong retail investor growth: 5+ crore demat accounts in India post-COVID
- Key challenges identified:
  - 90% of F&O traders lose money
  - Platform credibility at risk if recommendations fail
  - Need for proper research basis vs speculation
- Diversification opportunities beyond stocks:
  - Bonds, commodities, small case integration
  - Different products for different investor segments (retail vs HNI)

segments.speaker: Unknown
segments.raw_text:
- Positive reception to advisory-to-execution platform idea
  - Addresses gap between WhatsApp/Telegram recommendations and actual trading
  - Provides tracking and credibility vs unauthorized channels
- Suggested categorization approach:
  - Different customer segments for each timeframe
- Revenue model validation: Platform fees + advisor subscription model viable

segments.speaker: Unknown
segments.raw_text:
- Shashank Agarwal, Research Analyst (SEBI registered)
- Education: Calcutta University (Accounts & Finance), Delhi Institute of Management (Masters in Finance)
- Experience: ISEC Securities (equity advisory), Mutilal Oswal, Angel One
  - Built Angel One’s customer onboarding platform and transaction execution system
  - Developed entire tech stack using Python
- Current role: Wealth management (not stock picking)
  - Focus on mutual funds, bonds, CDs, PMS, AIF products
  - Avoids direct buy/sell calls due to client leverage risks

segments.speaker: Unknown
segments.raw_text:
- Terminal integration approach recommended over website-only
  - Real-time signals with buy/sell/target/stop-loss
  - API integration with existing broker platforms
  - Plugin/widget model for seamless execution
- Existing solutions reference:
  - Motilal Oswal’s trade guide signals (₹15,000/year subscription)
  - Value Research, Morning Star for stock recommendations
- Revenue streams: Subscription model (monthly/quarterly/yearly) + transaction-based fees
